It is proposed to exclude the installation of fire alarm speakers from the in-patient suite out of consideration for the patients well-being. The code requirements pertaining to audibility of fire alarms are to ensure that building occupants can evacuate the premises upon activation of an alarm. However, Building H is of B1 occupancy and patients cannot evacuate without being accompanied by trained personnel. It is paramount that fire alarm signals are audible for the building staff, but it is different for the patients as during the activation of the fire alarm system they cannot leave their suites, thus reducing the practicality of meeting audibility levels in patient suites brought forth by the 2024 OBC.

Alternative solution to exclude firefighter handsets within the floor area of the Phase 1D Forensics Building (Building H) for the Centre for Addiction and Mental Health (CAMH)

Due to the detention occupancy in the building, security and access control are critical operations which must be actively managed to support patient, staff, and public safety. Mandatory project specific operational requirements for the project identified that prescribed locking hardware needs to be either Electric Latch Retraction (ELR) Locksets, or Electric Strike (ES). It is understood that operationally this is critical to CAMH as it limits the risk of actuation delay that can lead to injury when responding to or fleeing a dangerous situation. The intent of the Alternative Solution is to outline an approach to the use of Electric Latch Retraction Locksets and Electric Strikes within the context of this building and its unique operational requirements and in alignment with the intent of the OBC with respect to occupant safety.

The station buildings will beconstructed as an elevated guideway on an embankment adjacent to the existing GO corridor. The site is bounded by Queen, Degrassi andStrange Streets as well as the GO embankment. The station will be accessible from Queen Street, Degrassi Street and Strange Street. There isan existing Overhead Catenary System (OCS) infrastructure in place as part of the local Street Cars transportation network and the OCS islocated beneath the existing GO corridor and the Station Buildings.
